feedback.text: it would be nice to ensure if you buy a game that has an offline component that the company cannot delete that game after they stop supporting it. this is the whole campaign around "stop killing games" and it would fit quite well into this digital fairness act. if companies want to sell a game that will stop working after they stop supporting it they should clearly label it as a service and what you're buying is a license to play the game and not the game itself. that way consumers are informed of what they are buying. but it would also be nice to ensure games can keep running after the company stops supporting them from a digital archive and legacy perspective. in the future these games are going to be similar to ancient books that should be persevered for future generations.
